prs.title: Fix Error on inverted range for _all_docs
prs.body:
## Overview

If a _view has an impossible start_key/end_key combination, CouchDB returns a helpful 400 query_parse_error. However, _all_docs lacked this validation and would silently return 200 with an empty result set {"rows":[]}.

## Testing recommendations

You can verify this locally using curl:

Before this PR (returns 200 with empty rows): curl -X GET "http://127.0.0.1:5984/mydb/_all_docs?startkey=\"z\"&endkey=\"a\""

After this PR (returns 400): curl -X GET "http://127.0.0.1:5984/mydb/_all_docs?startkey=\"z\"&endkey=\"a\""

## Related Issues or Pull Requests

[issue #5866](https://github.com/apache/couchdb/issues/5866) 

## Checklist

- [ ] This is my own work, I did not use AI, LLM's or similar technology
- [☑️] Code is written and works correctly
- [☑️] Changes are covered by tests
- [ ] Any new configurable parameters are documented in `rel/overlay/etc/default.ini`
- [ ] Documentation changes were made in the `src/docs` folder
- [ ] Documentation changes were backported (separated PR) to affected branches

prs.title: Added Google Drive integration for saving and opening diagrams
prs.body:
Fixes: [#573](https://github.com/drawdb-io/drawdb/issues/573)  

This PR adds Google Drive integration to DrawDB, streamlining the workflow for users who store their diagrams in the cloud.

Key Features:
Save to Google Drive: Allows users to save their current diagram directly to their Google Drive as a .ddb file.
Open from Google Drive: Enables users to browse their Google Drive and open supported diagram files directly within the editor.

prs.title: Fix: Add separate Line2 thresholds for world and pixel units
prs.body:
Fixes [#30623](https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js/issues/30623)

This PR addresses an issue where `Line2` objects relied on a single `threshold` parameter for raycasting, which caused inconsistent interaction behavior when switching between world units and pixel units. A threshold value suitable for world space (e.g., 0.5 units) is often too small or too large when interpreted as screen pixels.

I have updated [LineSegments2.js](and the WebGPU equivalent) to support two new parameters in `raycaster.params.Line2`:
*   `thresholdWorld`: Used when `worldUnits` is `true`.
*   `thresholdPixel`: Used when `worldUnits` is `false`.

prs.title: Fix: prevent unnecessary webgl canvas recreation in noSmooth()
prs.body:
Fixes: [#7548 ](https://github.com/processing/p5.js/issues/7548)


 Changes:
I fixed a bug where calling noSmooth() in WebGL mode would unnecessarily recreate the canvas, causing it to lose its CSS positioning. The fix involves checking the actual WebGL context attributes to see if antialiasing is already disabled before attempting to disable it again.

- [☑️] `npm run lint` passes

prs.title: Import exhibitor plugin with preserved git history
prs.body:
This PR imports the exhibitor plugin from the standalone "eventyay-tickets-exhibitors" repository into the main 'eventyay-tickets' repository while preserving complete git history.

Source Repository: https://github.com/yaxit24/eventyay-tickets-exhibitors.git
Branch: master

What I did: I followed the same process that was used for importing the talk component - basically a clean git merge that preserves all the original history while organizing everything into the exhibitor/ directory.

Want to double-check it's verbatim? Here's how:
 Quick verification - compare the original repo with what's imported
git clone https://github.com/yaxit24/eventyay-tickets-exhibitors.git temp-check
cd temp-check

 The file structure should match (just moved into exhibitor/ folder)
 Original: exhibitors/, tests/, pyproject.toml, README.rst
 Imported: exhibitor/exhibitors/, exhibitor/tests/, exhibitor/pyproject.toml, etc.

Git history check:
 This should show all the original commits from the exhibitor repo
git log --oneline exhibitor/exhibitors/models.py

## Summary by Sourcery

Import the Exhibitors plugin into the main repository, integrating its full codebase, models, API, control views, templates, migrations, tests, and packaging configuration.

New Features:
- Add Exhibitor plugin models for managing exhibitors, items, leads, and tags with key and booth ID generation
- Implement REST API endpoints for exhibitor authentication, info retrieval, item assignments, lead creation, retrieval, and updates
- Introduce Pretix control views and templates for creating, editing, listing, and deleting exhibitors and configuring plugin settings
- Register plugin routes in URLs and extend Pretix navigation via signals and PluginConfig
- Bundle packaging setup including pyproject.toml, setup.cfg, setup.py, MANIFEST.in, and scripts

Enhancements:
- Preserve full git history during plugin import and integrate packaging and localization infrastructure

Build:
- Include Makefile and scripts for localization and git hooks

CI:
- Add GitHub workflows for code style checks, plugin tests, and PyPI releases

Tests:
- Add pytest tests for CRUD operations on ExhibitorInfo

prs.title: feat(checkout-api): implement event-level checkout endpoint and improve search validation
prs.body:
Fixes [#822](https://github.com/fossasia/eventyay-tickets/issues/822). also refer the issue [#38 ](https://github.com/fossasia/eventyay-checkin/issues/38) in Checkin repo.

## Summary by Sourcery

Add an endpoint for event-wide attendee checkout and strengthen search behavior in check-in API, alongside CORS and base path configuration updates for testing.

New Features:
- Implement EventCheckoutView endpoint to check out all attendees of an event
- Add URL route for the new event checkout endpoint

Enhancements:
- Require non-empty search term and minimum length for checkin searches without full access
- Set BASE_PATH to empty string instead of default
- Introduce CORS middleware and allow all origins for testing

prs.title: Checkout api enhancement
prs.body:
Fixes #38.

## Summary by Sourcery

Enhance the Eventyay search check-in component and its Pinia store to support both check-in and check-out workflows, including single and bulk check-out, with improved UI feedback via popups and badge preview

New Features:
- Add check-out mode toggle and support for exiting attendees via the check-in/redeem API
- Implement an event-wide “Checkout All Attendees” action with confirmation dialog

Enhancements:
- Show attendee info popup with countdown timer, note input, and badge generation preview using StandardButton and BadgePrintPreview components
- Unify check-in and check-out flows in processEventyayCheckIn store with new isCheckoutMode, toggleMode, and eventCheckout actions
- Resume QR camera scanning after each operation

prs.title: Feature standalone popup
prs.body: Changes made: I've added a standalone tab to the SCRUM Helper extension, enabling users to generate and copy reports directly from the popup without needing email clients. The implementation includes a clean, user-friendly interface with a text area for editing reports and buttons for convenient report generation and clipboard copying. The design follows the extension's existing style patterns, ensuring a seamless experience while giving users more flexibility in how they create and share their SCRUM reports. Screenshots for the change: ## Summary by Sourcery Add a standalone report generation tab to the SCRUM Helper extension, allowing users to create and copy reports directly from the popup New Features: - Introduce a new 'Standalone' tab in the extension popup that enables users to generate and copy SCRUM reports without needing an email client Enhancements: - Modify the existing tab layout to accommodate the new standalone report feature - Implement report generation and clipboard copying functionality within the popup Chores: - Update the extension's user interface to support a third tab - Add event listeners for the new standalone report functionality

prs.body: Changes made: Implemented color transitions from gray to blue when checkboxes are checked/unchecked to provide clear visual feedback For the checkbox color transitions from gray to blue, I aimed to give a subtle yet clear visual. The label text starts as Grey then transitions smoothly to Blue, and have also increased the font size to 500. Screenshots for the change: ## Summary by Sourcery Enhance user interface by adding visual feedback for checkbox interactions through color and styling transitions New Features: - Add visual state changes for checkboxes, including color transition from gray to blue and font weight adjustment Enhancements: - Implement smooth color and styling transitions for checkboxes to improve user interaction feedback

prs.title: Create sso-oauth-configuration.md
prs.body: This PR adds a single new file that provides comprehensive instructions for setting up Single Sign-On (SSO) with GitHub, Google, and Wikimedia in pretix. The documentation includes step-by-step configuration instructions, example values, navigation guidance, and testing procedures to help users implement OAuth-based authentication for their pretix shops.

prs.title: Fix spelling in user settings and add SSO documentation (closes #475)
prs.body:
Fixed Spelling in User Settings and Added SSO Documentation:

Changed "Enabled" → "Enable" and "Disabled" → "Disable" in the two-factor authentication status labels.

Created a new guide explaining how to set up social logins (Wikimedia, GitHub, Google).

Included step-by-step instructions for creating OAuth apps and configuring them in pretix.

prs.title: Update pyproject.toml
prs.body:
production dependencies remain unchanged, dev tools are split into separate docs, test, and lint groups, and Docker/CI scripts are updated to use uv for installing Python libraries.

Moved development libraries out of the single "dev" feature and split them into three groups:
docs: For Sphinx and other libraries needed to build the documentation.
test: For pytest and related testing tools (we'll add mypy and stubs later).
lint: For linting tools like Ruff.
Switched our dependency management to uv for faster and more reliable version resolution.
Updated our Docker build scripts and GitHub Actions workflows to use uv commands (e.g., uv sync) instead of pip, since pip can’t handle uv’s lock file.

## Summary by Sourcery

Refactor development dependencies into separate groups for documentation, testing, and linting. Migrate to uv for dependency management, updating Docker build scripts and CI workflows accordingly.

Enhancements:
- Split development libraries into docs, test, and lint groups.
- Migrate to uv for dependency management for faster and more reliable version resolution.
- Update Docker build scripts and GitHub Actions workflows to use uv commands instead of pip, since pip can’t handle uv’s lock file.
- Remove pydantic dependency

prs.title: Update pyproject.toml
prs.body:
Here the updated configuration is ready for uv to manage dependencies.
Production libraries remain in the main dependencies list, while development tools have been moved from the dev feature (in [project.optional-dependencies]) into a dedicated dependency group ([project.dependency-groups.dev]). This separation is what uv (and modern practices) recommend.

here please let me know for any further changes and correction made, Also being new to open source, please suggest me with projects and issues to contribute.

## Summary by Sourcery

Enhancements:
- Separates development dependencies into a dedicated dependency group.

prs.title: Update Navbar.css #260
prs.body: Changed z-index of navbar Fixes # The change involves using z-index to position the ElementsPanel on top of the Navbar, allowing it to be freely movable without being obstructed by the Navbar. The Navbar's z-index was changed from 100 to 60 to ensure that it stays below the ElementsPanel. This adjustment guarantees that the ElementsPanel with a higher z-index (such as 99) remains on top, allowing it to be draggable and fully interactive without being obstructed by the Navbar. This change resolves the overlap issue and improves the overall user interface functionality. ### Screenshots of the changes (If any) - ## Summary by CodeRabbit - **Style** - Updated navbar stacking order with a modified z-index value

prs.title: Update Navbar.css
prs.body: Enhanced UI Flexibility: Updated the ElementsPanel to always appear above the Navbar using a higher z-index, ensuring uninterrupted drag-and-drop functionality. Fixes # have made changes in the src/components/Navbar/Navbar.css file in the z- index. here by changeing the z- index there will solve the problem of the drag-and-drop functionality when the Elementspanel (project-properties, circuit-elements,etc) when dragged to the top, there top part get hidden behind the navbar. i have decreased the z index to the navbar than that of the Elementspanel, because of which while overlapping the Elementspanel would be in the front of the navbar, hence have solved the problem. ## Summary by CodeRabbit - **Style** - Adjusted navbar z-index to improve layering of page elements

- **Documentation**

prs.body: ## What does this PR do? The changes made enhance user-friendliness and interactivity by adding emojis for visual appeal, organizing content with clear structure and friendly language, highlighting key features, including interactive links to resources, and incorporating visuals like images and GIFs for better understanding. please give me feedback and any changes to be made. Fixes # (issue) The changes address issues of engagement and accessibility by making the content more visually appealing and easier to navigate, thereby improving user experience and comprehension. ## Type of change - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] This change requires a documentation update ## Mandatory Tasks - [ ] Make sure you have self-reviewed the code. A decent size PR without self-review might be rejected. ## Checklist ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Enhances `README.md` with emojis, visuals, and interactive links for improved user engagement and accessibility. > > - **Visual Enhancements**: > - Added emojis for visual appeal and section headers in `README.md`. > - Incorporated images and GIFs for better understanding and engagement. > - **Content Organization**: > - Structured content with clear headings and friendly language. > - Highlighted key features and sections for easier navigation. > - **Interactive Elements**: > - Included interactive links to resources, demos, and documentation. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=langfuse%2Flangfuse&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 3a486aecf30e8aa1fe6712c25d365c6f9067ca1f. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

prs.title: Update README.md
prs.body:
Changes made to the README file include the addition of emojis to enhance visual appeal and engagement. Key sections like Introduction, Getting Started, How to Contribute, and Licenses now feature relevant emojis, making the document more inviting and easier to navigate. Additionally, GIFs or images could be included to illustrate important concepts, further enriching the user experience. This approach not only makes the README more attractive but also improves its clarity and accessibility.

<!--
Thank you for contributing to Java Design Patterns!

If you're unsure where to start, please refer to the contributing doc:

https://github.com/iluwatar/java-design-patterns/wiki/01.-How-to-contribute

If you still have questions, please let us know via issues or [gitter](https://matrix.to/#/#iluwatar_java-design-patterns:gitter.im).
-->

## What problem does this PR solve?

Adding emojis and visuals to the README file enhances user engagement and navigability, effectively addressing issues of clarity and appeal. This makes it easier for users to understand key sections and encourages contributions, ultimately improving the overall user experience.

prs.title: Update README.md
prs.body: my chances will make the coordinators identity hidden.

issues.title: API to Create New Orders for On-Site Attendees
issues.body:
To support on-site attendee registration, introduce an API that allows event staff to create new ticket orders directly from the check-in application.
This enhancement will streamline last-minute registrations at event venues especially in cases where attendees register at the counter using cash payments or through manual confirmation methods.

issues.title: Add Roles to Attendees and Matching Roles with Products & Badges
issues.body:
Organizers can assign roles (like speaker, volunteer, exhibitor, sponsor) to attendees, either when buying tickets or later.

Automatic role assignment – Certain tickets automatically set a role (e.g., buying a "VIP ticket" assigns VIP role).

Filtering & exporting – Easily get lists or data of only certain roles (e.g., all volunteers).

issues.title: API to enable Checkout
issues.body: Apply the required backend modifications to support the checkout-api integration in the Checkin application.

issues.title: API to enable Checkout
issues.body:
API to enable Checkout for an event (whole event!)
Create an API That will handle checkout for the whole event. (eventyay-tickets and in the checkin-app. In the checkin staff page we need a toggle to active checkin or checkout.)

issues.title: Clear error message from Check-In API
issues.body: To make the changes to display the Error messages, it is part of  the issue[ #809](https://github.com/fossasia/eventyay-tickets/issues/809) in Eventyay-Tickets.

issues.title: Clear error message from Check-In API
issues.body: When the user is checked in there are error messages (e.g. the user is already checked in), it does not prevent them from checking in again. Often the reason why the checkin has failed is not mentioned. Goal: We need displays of error. Also need to take care of things like “User cannot be checked in. User is already checked in.”

issues.title: To Add the Sub-Category Feature and Badge Type feature
issues.body: Implement a feature to support sub-categories. The primary purpose is to assign these categories and sub-categories to products (tickets) to define a specific badge type, such as 'Sponsor' or 'Student.' Also these used categories and sub-categories should be used to to define the badge type.

issues.title: To Add Backside Edit Feature in the Badge PDF Editor.
issues.body:
To add the functionality here to edit  the back-side of the badges, in the editor as shown in the video of the ZOHO Backstage.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ec2a167-fa98-41e1-9d11-9a9dd31729b8

issues.title: Add Feature For Badge Type
issues.body: Make as shown here in the images, where each badge will have its type, like Exibitors, Sponsor, staff, Organiser, Speaker etc.

issues.title: Add The Text Content Functionality
issues.body:
Issue: Currently to add a text field here we use the "text" but it has a default placeholder value that is "Product Name" where it should be like that on clicking a place holder value, a new field should appear on the badge template. 
The feature should be as: 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/17e53b3b-ddfb-4fbe-9700-fb39c063c5b1

issues.title: Bug: Badge Editor Layout Does Not Fit Within a Single Desktop Screen
issues.body: The current badge editor layout does not fit entirely within a single desktop screen. This fragmented view makes it harder for users to design and visualize badges effectively, especially when frequent scrolling or switching between preview modes is required.

issues.title: Limit Default Text Placeholders to Organizer-Selected Fields
issues.body:
The Badges plugin currently includes 58 default text placeholders. To streamline the badge creation process, we propose limiting the default placeholders to only the information that organizers select as required for their event.

Enhancement :
To solve the issue as by creating the two sub-menu for the placeholder value, into 'Users info' and 'Events info' here with the default 5-10 values for each, like here in the image of the badge creator of the ZOHO.

issues.title: The Badge page is accessible without enabling the extension.
issues.body: The "/tickets/common/event/{organiser-name}/{event-name}/badge" page is accessible without enabling the Badge extension at "/tickets/control/event/{organiser-name}/{event-name}/settings/plugins". Without enabling the extension: With enabling the extension:

issues.title: Functional standalone pop-up
issues.body: Include a fully functional standalone pop-up interface for users who prefer not to rely on specific email providers.
